item.title: High-agency strategy
item.description: Matt Ström-Awn · Designer, leader, and coach focused on building exceptional products and teams.

item.title: Claude Code Isn't About Code
item.description: The people getting the most out of Claude Code aren't writing code. Some notes on where the value moved, picking up where the disposable tools manifesto left off.
item.author: (Vlad Temian)

item.title: The Abundance Mirage: why the road to hell is paved in unchallenged assumptions
item.description: In the whole wasteland of AI discourse, there is perhaps no more dangerous proposition than "Abundance is universally good", a statement that is almost too tautological to be challenged. To illustrate my point, I'm going to walk you through a thought exercise in designing a dictatorship from the ground up without employing any obvious mechanisms for oppression.

item.title: Building Analytics for Haxball, a Game I’ve Played Since 2011
item.description: I built a live and historical analytics dashboard for Haxball that turns match data into player stats, team stats, charts, summaries, and xG.

item.title: The Permanent Underclass
item.description: There’s this phrase that’s taking root in our Silicon Valley discourse. It drives me nuts and I’m making a clarion call for us to push back against its toxic ideology. It’s The Permanent Underclass. I can’t say how widely the phrase has spread but in my corner of the world - Silicon Valley, Technology - it comes up often enough that I assume most of my peers have at least heard it even if they are not regularly discussing it.

item.title: Good Agents
item.description: The spirit of Radical Speed Month includes an impetus to experiment. So experiment I have.

item.title: Why I’m Skeptical About Efforts to Revolutionize Schooling - Scott H Young
item.description: A brief explanation of my pessimism about efforts to radically improve education.

item.title: charles leifer | Cave of Forgotten Dreams
item.description:
June 02, 2026 10:08 /
ai
thoughts
/ 0 comments

item.title: Should CEOs Fire Their Software Developers? | Spicer Matthews
item.description: CEOs are getting AI-pilled and asking why they still pay developers. After advising dozens of leaders, here's my honest framework for who should hire and who should YOLO.
item.author: (Spicer Matthews)

item.title: Web3 means Freedom Software
item.description: Web3's promise of user sovereignty remains unfulfilled—corrupted by venture-driven growth pressures that have strayed far from its original vision. True web3 must embody censorship resistance, open source freedom, privacy, and security, principles

item.title: Review of the MoErgo Go60 Keyboard
item.description:
It’s time for another keyboard review.
Last year, I reviewed the MoErgo Glove80. It was and is still one of my most visited blog posts.In that review, I compared it to the Kinesis Advantage360 Pro and wrote about the travel case, design, switches, tenting, ZMK configuration, community, and

item.title: Anthropic Whales Dominate Usage Ahead of IPO - Keith Brown
item.description: The insights, analysis, and data presented in this article are for informational and educational purposes only and do not constitute financial, investment, legal, or tax advice. Always conduct your own independent due diligence before buying or selling any security. I was catching up with the founder of CostHawk recently, asking him what he’s seeing in […]
item.author: (https://www.facebook.com/heykeithbrown)

item.title: Clickhouse table per tenant in production · Anantha Kumaran
item.description: At work, we made a decision to go with a table per tenant approach and we have been running that setup for a couple of years now. I thought this would be the right time to share what we have learned and what works well.

item.title: Inherited the frontend of a 150K-user app, mostly vibe coded - Kool Codez
item.description: The product runs the whole business and has 150K users. The codebase underneath needs a lot of work. Where do you draw the line on quality when this many people depend on it?
item.author: (Lucio D'Alessandro)

item.title: The Internet Needs More Cross-Pollinators
item.description: So, my silly little fanfic project blew up like crazy and received some really negative feedback. And I think I understand why. Exploring the concept of boundary spanning and cross-pollination in online communities. Drawing on organizational theory and the work of Michael Tushman. We need people who move between different online subcultures to seed ideas and build bridges.
item.author: (Brennan Kenneth Brown)

item.title: Arcan: 10 Years of Online Obscurity
item.description: … and 23 years of tedious yak shaving. Ten years ago my rather anemic post on the Arcan project started to circulate. It got enough attention that I considered stopping right there and …

item.title: The LLM Job Paradox
item.description: By this I mean, do you think your entire job could be done by an AI. Can your entire team / field / department be removed without issue?

item.title: Self-extensible software is here and its amazing | Slopfluencer
item.description: Slopfluencer: posts on model training, evaluations, agents, NLP, and building products with generative AI.

item.title: Anthropic and the caravel problem · Valentin Radu
item.description: In 1500 a country of a million people invented the ship that opened the world, got rich beyond its size, and then lost everything to latecomers with deeper pockets. Frontier AI labs are sailing the same caravel. Why the model was never the moat, and the two narrow ways out.

item.title: Claude Code from the beach: My remote coding setup with mosh, tmux and ntfy | rogs
Introducing MAI-Code-1-Flash | Microsoft AI
item.description: Coding models are most useful when they perform well in the same environment developers use every day. That is why we built MAI-Code-1-Flash with production workflows at the center, rather than optimizing only for benchmarks. The model was trained directly with GitHub Copilot harnesses used in production. This allows it to learn how to interact with surrounding tools and systems in agentic coding tasks, making it uniquely well suited to real-world Copilot workflows compared to other available models.

item.title: Quote of the day by Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison: "Citizens will be on their best behavior, because we’re constantly recording and reporting everything that is going on" — a dire warning on the erosion of privacy
item.description: Legendary technologist Larry Ellison has outlined a vision for an increasingly aggressive surveillance state in the 21st century
item.author: (https://www.techradar.com/author/keumars-afifi-sabet)

item.title: Gmail Thinks I'm Stupid, So I Left
item.description: Gmail's rapid descent into belittling their users, powered by AI.

item.title: A walking tour of surveillance infrastructure in Seattle
item.description: coveillance.org

item.title: Adafruit Industries - Makers, hackers, artists, designers and engineers!
item.description: electronics, open source hardware, hacking and more...

item.title: GitHub - cyberpapiii/chipotlai-max: The AI coding agent that runs on stolen Chipotle compute 🌯 Fork of OpenCode with Pepper AI as default model. Community project to add providers from Home Depot, Lowes, Target, Starbucks & more.
item.description: The AI coding agent that runs on stolen Chipotle compute 🌯 Fork of OpenCode with Pepper AI as default model. Community project to add providers from Home Depot, Lowes, Target, Starbucks & more....

item.title: Age verification for social media – the beginning of the end for a free internet? | Mullvad VPN
item.description: So-called age verification for social media is spreading across the world, framed as an effort to create a safer internet for children. In reality, age verification lays the foundation for a fully government controlled internet.

item.title: macOS Needs Its Grid Back
item.description: Two decades ago I had a better Mac desktop experience than I have today. I only had a single low res (by todays standards) screen, yet I felt like Hugh Jackman in Swordfish - deftly navigating more than nine displays without thinking, muscle and spatial memory working seamlessly together. TLDR; I built an app to return macOS spaces to its Pre-Lion Grid-enabled Glory. Read on for the increasingly rare experience of an actual human dropping a bit of nostalgia, the thinking behind why make this and some issues encountered along the way.

item.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
item.description: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now generally available on AWS, giving enterprises a new path to build with OpenAI through the AWS environments, controls, and procurement workflows they already use. Customers can get started with OpenAI on AWS and move faster from evaluation to production.
